mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) increases aggregate wage income and consumer spending, which lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5% signals an aggressive monetary easing stance that compresses the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds at 0.2% as of April 2010
failure_condition: Months with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in the majority of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals a broad-based demand recovery in which supply and demand expansion reinforce each other, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint upward moves in INDPRO and RSAFS fail to precede positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During high-unemployment regimes, a monthly decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals tightening labor-market slack that accelerates consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, month-over-month declines in UNRATE are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above a 2.5% annualized threshold pressures the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following high CPI readings above the 2.5% threshold are positive in more than 55% of triggered observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) lead construction activity and durable goods demand, transmitting into broad economic growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a productivity-driven expansion where output grows without labor market overheating, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising INDPRO and flat-or-falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of occurrences over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) growth above 2% annualized signals robust aggregate demand that outpaces expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions track the output gap.
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth exceeding 2% annualized are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.
